title: Tomb - Overview and Solo Play Development
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yKIM1UxtbpU

Daniel (@dungeondive) digs into TOMB, John Zinser's competitive party-building dungeon crawler from AEG, and the homebrew solo variant he's been hammering into shape. His verdict on the box as printed is blunt: a terrible rulebook and near-nonexistent solo rules wrapped around one of the most lavishly illustrated games he owns — stacks of encounter, treasure, trap, curse and character cards, each with unique credited art. The competitive game (build a party, raid static tomb boards, backstab rivals for the highest XP) he rates merely okay and overlong, but he's convinced a great game is "bubbling just underneath the surface."

Most of the runtime is Daniel walking through his solo fix: drawing a party of four from a bag of twenty expendable heroes, equipping them by class (fighters draw items, clerics prayers, rogues tactics, wizards spells), and clearing every crypt without burning through all twenty. To give the otherwise-pointless movement some teeth he adds two roaming monster markers that chase the party, plus a d6-triggered Crypt Master card to ramp difficulty. He readily admits it isn't finished — many PvP-flavoured abilities still need on-the-fly reinterpretation — and openly invites viewers to develop the variant further. A candid look at salvaging a flawed-but-gorgeous game for the solo table.

----

TOMB is the game Daniel says he'd most like to "fix" because so much good is already in the box. What's a game in your collection that you keep house-ruling because you can feel a great design buried inside it?
